hypothesis: If a language model is fine-tuned on BCC/B2 alloy data and then optimized with Direct Preference Optimization against a Thermo-Calc-based hierarchical reward that enforces phase coexistence, BCC-first formation, room-temperature B2 presence, and <10% non-BCC/B2 phases, then the resulting designs will achieve higher mean reward scores and greater compositional validity, coverage, and novelty than baseline methods, because DPO aligns generated outputs with the specified physics-based synthesis objectives.
